transcript.clean_text: laying claim to being Australia's oldest and most mountainous stage race the Jacob herald-sun tour also boasts an honor roll that includes the likes of Simon Gerrans Bradley Wiggins and Chris Froome this year we've got four World Tour teams mixing it with some informed continental squads over five stages the first of which starts from the mitchelton winery in the Gambian takes in one hundred and twenty one point nine flat kilometres to Shepperton not much to dissuade the sprinters from taking an early shot at the overall lead but the exposed nature of these country roads could lead to some potential crosswinds if they blow strong enough with the first intermediate sprint position just nine point five kilometers into the state it was no surprise that the race was still all together when we got there Roy evening of Sapporo cycling taking the bonuses from Nick white and Craig Wiggins who's no relation by the way the fight to be in the main break way of the day was just beginning at this point while a few World Tour jerseys were up there in the mix it was initially two groups of two that came together at the front they being Conor Mirta of Oliver's real food racing Ben Hill a team bridge lane Shell's Etienne Collett Ian of a Volo and Ryan Thomas of ära Sunshine Coast the bunch were more than happy to fan out and out at least for the time being everyone that is except for Jesse Coyle of near a continental missing the initial jump but chasing solo for quite some time to British the leaders and it's hard to tell if he thought the reward was worth the effort at this point to be honest back in the peloton the big names were happy to set the early tempo and with a fairly Swift pace taking them quicker than the expected time schedule set by the organisers then won three minutes or so was afforded to this motivated quintet mitchelton Scots had committed Sam Beauty to the head of the peloton with about 30 kilometers to go and this was the point at which that competitive lead began to dwindle still some bonuses on offer at the second intermediate sprint though and another hotly contested once all been he'll take the time bonuses 29 year old Australian was in a bullish mood today continuing his effort through the line and not content to sit up just yet well the prospect of yet another chase was just too much for Coyle the first of the break to succumb to the pace is murder pressed on to rejoin the head of affairs with the peloton in sight now there weren't that many more places to run but that didn't stop this last stitch attack from the young Canadian klett Ian and this actually reinvigorated him into charging on again no doubt who would go to the podium as the most combative rider at the end of the day with just over five kilometers to go and we were finally all together for the anticipated bunch Sprint's the team of the fast men as always fighting for position this time for a tight final corner inside the final 500 metres Alberta done AZ of Sun Webb Moreno hofland of EF pro cycling and Kane groves of Michels and Scott among the victim tenders as you could see their teams were well represented towards the front to do all they could to deliver the win there is that right-hander in the distance everybody's strung out to get around in one piece and making it through in perfect position with the aforementioned trio and it was the young Italian Daisy who got his nose in front to take his first win of the season slow mo shows him getting into an almost UN esque sprinting position to get the better of his former s e-g Racing teammate Caden grows both who look like superb prospects making their way at World Tour level this year look down the top-10 C's hoffland round out the podium and third make out Ryman for for Israel start-up nation with another youngster Godfrey Slattery grabbing fit for the Australian national team nice little bonus of the overall leader's jersey for Daisy but with the summit finish is starting as early as tomorrow it could be a fairly brief stint in yellow for it time bonuses in place so that's ten six and four seconds off to the first three riders to cross the line with the riders up there in the intermediate sprints earlier in the day nudging themselves up the overall ranks - please do join us for stage two tomorrow and don't forget you can watch all the stages live here on GC and racing including the women's herald-sun tour - with highlights as well in English and Spanish every day if you just want to catch up you
